name: Broccoli
summary: A cruciferous vegetable that's both a strong vitamin C and fiber source and the best-known dietary source of sulforaphane.
superfoodReason: Combines high vitamin C and fiber with glucoraphanin, the precursor to sulforaphane — a compound under active clinical study for its effects on inflammation and metabolism.

benefits.claim: Delivers sulforaphane, a well-studied plant compound
benefits.mechanism: When you chop or chew broccoli, the enzyme myrosinase converts its glucoraphanin into sulforaphane, which activates the body's Nrf2 pathway — switching on antioxidant and detoxification genes. Human trials are exploring effects on inflammation and metabolic markers.

citations.title: Clinical and molecular evidence of the consumption of broccoli, glucoraphanin and sulforaphane in humans (Yagishita et al., Nutrients 2015)

benefits.claim: High in vitamin C and fiber
benefits.mechanism: Broccoli rivals citrus for vitamin C — supporting immune function and collagen — and its fiber feeds gut bacteria and supports regularity.

citations.title: Vitamin C — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

benefits.claim: Helps the body clear inhaled air pollutants
benefits.mechanism: Sulforaphane switches on the NRF2 pathway, which ramps up the detoxification enzymes (glutathione S-transferases) that tag toxins for excretion. In a randomized trial in a heavily polluted region of China, a daily broccoli-sprout beverage measurably sped up the body's excretion of inhaled pollutants such as benzene and acrolein — of interest to ex-smokers and people in dirty air.

citations.title: Rapid and sustainable detoxication of airborne pollutants by broccoli sprout beverage: results of a randomized clinical trial in China (Egner et al., Cancer Prev Res 2014)

surprisingFacts.text: The enzyme that makes sulforaphane is destroyed by heat, so hard-boiled broccoli makes little — chopping it and letting it rest, or a quick steam, preserves far more.
